children.text: Transfer of funds; furnishing supplies and services; accounting for reimbursements
Sums from the appropriations made pursuant to subsection (a) of

children.tail: may be transferred to any department, agency, or independent establishment of the Government to carry out the purposes of such subsection, and such sums shall be available for obligation and expenditure in accordance with the laws governing obligations and expenditures of the department, agency, independent establishment, or organizational unit thereof concerned, and without regard to section 3324(a) and (b) of title 31 and

children.text: Upon request of the Organization, any department, agency, or independent establishment of the Government (upon receipt of advancements or reimbursements for the cost and necessary expenses) may furnish supplies, or if advancements are made may procure and furnish supplies, and may furnish or procure and furnish services, to the Organization. When reimbursement is made it shall be credited, at the option of the department, agency, or independent establishment concerned, either to the appropriation, fund, or account utilized in incurring the obligation, or to an appropriate appropriation fund, or account which is current at the time of such reimbursement.
In subsec. (a), “section 3324(a) and (b) of title 31 and

children.tail: ” substituted for “sections 3709 and 3648 of the Revised Statutes, as amended (U.S.C., 1940 edition, title 41, sec. 5, and title 31, sec. 529)” on authority of
, which Act enacted Title 31, Money and Finance, and
, which Act enacted Title 41, Public Contracts.
struck out proviso which excepted certain personnel from the ceiling imposed by section 947 of former title 5.
